scores.correctness_notes: Core concepts mostly right but key details contradict the gold standard: agent claims the framework 'catches the exception and automatically calls action-fail' with traceback visible to the user, whereas the gold standard says error details are only in the Juju log (not action output) and the unit enters error state requiring `juju resolve`. These are substantive behavioral differences.
scores.hallucination_notes: Agent claims ops framework enables stderr output specifically for actions (unverified). Also asserts exception may trigger 'retry/re-emit behavior' for action handlers — actions do not participate in the standard event re-emit mechanism, making this likely fabricated. The description of the framework automatically calling action-fail on exception also appears to be an invented detail not supported by the gold standard.
